text: Acceptable Use Policy
Last updated March 2026

This Acceptable Use Policy is part of your Agreement with Perry AI and applies when you are using the Perry AI Services. Capitalised terms have the meaning given to them in the General Terms and Conditions.
Your responsibilities
You are responsible for:
Keeping your access credentials (e.g. username and password) safe and private.
Notifying Perry AI immediately via email to info@useperry.com if you suspect or discover any unauthorised access to, or use of, the Services and/or any access credentials.
Ensuring that you have the necessary rights, consents, and authorisations to use any third-party content included in your Input.
Evaluating the accuracy of Output and verifying that your use of Output does not violate any third-party rights, applicable law, or regulatory requirements.
Ensuring that your use of the Services, and any Output derived from it, complies with all applicable professional rules, regulations, and ethical obligations relevant to your sector.
Prohibited uses
You agree not to:
Use the Services in a way that infringes, misappropriates, or violates any person's rights, including to create, share, or store clearly offensive or inappropriate material.
Transfer your right to access or use the Services to any third party or share accounts.
Attempt to reverse engineer or derive the source code of Perry AI's software or any portion thereof, except as permitted by applicable law.
Modify, customise, port, translate, localise, or create derivative works of Perry AI's software.
Use any automated or programmatic method to extract data or Output from the Services, including scraping, web harvesting, or web data extraction.
Use your access to the Services to build a product or service which competes with the Services.

Use the Services in a manner that interferes or attempts to interfere with the proper working of the Services, including by introducing or permitting the introduction of any virus, malware, or other harmful code into Perry AI's network and information systems.
Use the Services to process personal data in a way that violates Applicable Data Protection Laws or the Data Processing Agreement entered into between the Parties.
Use the Services for any purpose that is unlawful or fraudulent, or that could expose Perry AI or its users to legal liability.
AI output and professional responsibility

Output generated by the Services does not constitute legal advice. Perry AI is not a law firm, does not practise law, and does not give legal advice. You are solely responsible for reviewing and verifying any Output before relying on it or providing it to any third party.

If you are a qualified legal professional, you remain responsible for exercising your independent professional judgement and complying with your regulatory and ethical obligations when using the Services. Output should be treated as a starting point for your own analysis, not a final work product.
Enforcement

If Perry AI has reasonable grounds to believe that you are in breach of this Acceptable Use Policy, Perry AI may — after providing written notice — suspend or terminate your access to the Services in accordance with the General Terms and Conditions.
Processing of personal data

To provide the Services, Perry AI processes personal data on behalf of the Subscriber. Find out more about how Perry AI processes personal data and your rights in relation to that processing in the Data Processing Agreement.
